title: 4. Let PQ and RS intersect at C. The point C will be the centre of the circle (Fig. 7.3).
subject: Mathematics
unit: Unit L - the other end to move freely along the circle
activityTitle: 4. Let PQ and RS intersect at C. The point C will be the centre of the circle (Fig. 7.3).
objective:
To find the values of sine and cosine
functions in second, third and fourth
quadrants using their given values in
first quadrant.
Cardboard, white chart paper, ruler,
coloured pens, adhesive, steel
wires and needle.
procedure:
1. Take a cardboard of convenient size and paste a white chart paper on it.
2. Draw a unit circle with centre O on chart paper.
3. Through the centre of the circle, draw two perpendicular lines   X OX ′   and
YOY ′   representing   x -axis and   y -axis, respectively, as shown in Fig.8.1.
observation:
Using thread, measure arc lengths RP, PS, RQ, QS and record them in the
table given below :

3. The value of one radian is equal to ________ degrees.
discussion: This result is useful in the study of trigonometric functions.
